acceptance_targets: [C01_CRAFT_BODY_PLAN_CONTRADICTION] A cold reader can describe Kai's installed hardware once, after reading the revised lines 5, 33, 81, and 105, without contradiction. Pass only if those lines support the same body plan and no later sentence implies an unestablished forearm graft or visible external bridge port.

[C01_DIALOGUE_STYLE_BIBLE_ECHO] Pass only if the revised lines 159 and 195 no longer use the 'hand ... already there' image or a close paraphrase, while a cold reader can still tell from those lines that Kai experiences execution arriving before conscious choice.

[C01_DIALOGUE_CLIPPED_TEXT_MESSAGE] Pass only if the first incoming text at line 67 is syntactically complete and immediately intelligible without the reader supplying missing words, and the surrounding exchange still reads as an ordinary mother-son text thread rather than exposition.

[C01_PROSE_HUM_DESCRIPTION_TIC] Pass only if no more than one revised passage uses the 'lived in the walls / below conversation / above silence' scaffold, and each remaining reference to facility noise adds a distinct new effect on Kai rather than restating the same acoustic impression.
[R028] Ch 1 contains no more than 8 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 95 prose lines).

findings.acceptance_test: A cold reader can describe Kai's installed hardware once, after reading the revised lines 5, 33, 81, and 105, without contradiction. Pass only if those lines support the same body plan and no later sentence implies an unestablished forearm graft or visible external bridge port.
findings.problem: Kai's enhancement layout changes inside the chapter: the setup frames his rebuild as leg grafts plus spinal bridge and visual processing, but the cafeteria beat suddenly gives him synthetic muscle in the forearm and the calibration scene introduces a visible bridge access port at a different body location. That breaks the continuity sheet's body plan and undermines trust in the chapter's core bodily-stakes reporting.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:81, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:105, pick one hardware configuration and keep it consistent. If canon remains leg-grafts-only, render the water-glass beat as predictive neural timing in a natural forearm and remove the visible external access-port description. If the forearm graft or exposed port is intentional, seed that hardware from its first mention and bring the canonical materials into alignment before release.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised lines 159 and 195 no longer use the 'hand ... already there' image or a close paraphrase, while a cold reader can still tell from those lines that Kai experiences execution arriving before conscious choice.
findings.problem: Kai's key articulation of the proprioception drift echoes the style bible's calibration-only 'your hand is already there' reference closely enough to read as lifted template language, then repeats the same image in the chapter's closing sting. Because the copied material sits in a chapter-defining beat and recurs, it damages voice integrity on first read.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the phrasing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:159 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:195 with fresh Kai-specific language. Keep the bodily problem legible, but do not reuse the style-bible image, syntax, or the 'already there' formulation; the replacement should feel like Kai building a concrete analogy in real time, not reciting a pre-calibrated line.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the first incoming text at line 67 is syntactically complete and immediately intelligible without the reader supplying missing words, and the surrounding exchange still reads as an ordinary mother-son text thread rather than exposition.
findings.problem: Kai's mother's opening text, 'Colder than you probably,' reads like accidental truncation rather than living shorthand. It lands as dropped words or process damage, not as characterful texting, which breaks the realism of an exchange that otherwise does useful social work.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67 in place, and lightly tune the adjacent exchange if needed at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:69 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71 so the first message is complete on first read while still sounding like Kai's mother texting casually.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if no more than one revised passage uses the 'lived in the walls / below conversation / above silence' scaffold, and each remaining reference to facility noise adds a distinct new effect on Kai rather than restating the same acoustic impression.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same marked acoustic scaffold four times: the facility noise 'lived' in the walls and sat below conversation or above silence. By the third use, the phrasing stops reading as deliberate motif and starts reading as an autopilot sentence template.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the ambient-noise descriptions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:25, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:89, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:189 so only one passage keeps this exact walls/silence formulation. Later mentions should either do new sensory work on Kai's body or be cut.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 1 contains no more than 8 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 95 prose lines).
findings.evidence: Ch 1: 35/95 prose lines contain em-dashes = 36.8%. Manuscript-wide: 654/1876 = 34.9% (threshold 10%, target <8%).
findings.problem: Ch 1 has em-dash density of 36.8%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 1 from 35 to ~8 (27 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
